info.title: Aynstyn API
info.description:
The Aynstyn API gives AI agents and developers programmatic access to Aynstyn's
AI-powered learning and assessment platform for competitive exams (UPSC, CAT, GATE,
GRE, NEET, JEE) and professional development.

## Authentication

Aynstyn supports two authentication methods:

### 1. Firebase Bearer Token (direct integration)
Obtain a Firebase ID token after signing in via Firebase Authentication.
Pass it as `Authorization: Bearer <idToken>` on every request.

### 2. OAuth 2.0 (recommended for third-party agents)
Use the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow to obtain an access token on behalf
of a user. Scope your request to only the permissions your agent needs.

**Authorization endpoint:** `https://app.aynstyn.com/api/oauth/authorize`
**Token endpoint:** `https://app.aynstyn.com/api/oauth/token`

## Scopes

| Scope | Description |
|---|---|
| `profile:read` | Read user profile and settings |
| `assessments:read` | View assessment history and results |
| `assessments:write` | Create and submit assessments |
| `learning:read` | View learning goals, courses and progress |
| `learning:write` | Create and update learning goals and courses |
| `interviews:read` | View interview history and available slots |
| `interviews:write` | Schedule and cancel interviews |
| `planner:read` | View planner tasks |
| `planner:write` | Create and manage planner tasks |
| `credits:read` | View credit balance and transaction history |

## Base URLs

| Environment | Base URL |
|---|---|
| Production | `https://app.aynstyn.com` |

## Credits

Most AI-powered operations consume credits. A standard text assessment costs 4 credits.
Credits are deducted after a successful response. Insufficient credits returns HTTP 402.

get.description: Returns authentication status and basic user info from the active session.

post.description: Invalidates the current session and clears auth cookies.

get.description:
Redirects the user to the Aynstyn consent page. After approval, the user
is redirected back to `redirect_uri` with an authorization code.

post.description: Exchange an authorization code for an access token, or refresh an existing token.

get.description: Returns basic identity info for the OAuth-authenticated user.

post.description: Called once after first Firebase sign-in to create the DB user record.

Runs an AI-powered assessment on a subject and returns a scored report with
topic-level feedback and improvement recommendations.

**Credits:** 4 credits per assessment (authenticated users). Anonymous users
have a daily attempt limit.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's past assessments ordered by date.
Returns remaining daily assessment attempts for unauthenticated users.

post.description:
Accepts a free-text learning objective. The AI breaks it into a structured
topic list with progress tracking.

201.description: Goal created with AI-generated topic plan

post.description: Creates a collaborative assessment with an invite link for team members.

bearerAuth.description:
Firebase ID token obtained after authenticating via Firebase Authentication.
Pass as `Authorization: Bearer <idToken>`.

oauth2.description:
OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. Obtain `client_id` and `client_secret`
by contacting support@aynstyn.com.

scopes.interviews:read: View interview history and available slots
